description.para: These constants specify the handler events that are passed to an application event handler from the driver.
These constants are bit masks such that more than one event can be passed to the handler.

briefdescription.para: Receive Buffer Underflow or Transmit buffer Overflow.

detaileddescription.para: These constants are used to pass into the device setup routines to set up the device according to transfer direction.Transfer direction is sending

briefdescription.para: Wait for 2 sec in worst case.
Maximum number of entries in the Device Address Table (DAT) supported by the controller and tracked by the driver.
Place one byte into the transmit FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is the instance of IIC
is the instance of IIC

briefdescription.para: The handler data type allows the user to define a callback function to respond to interrupt events in the system.

detaileddescription.para: This function is executed in interrupt context, so amount of processing should be minimized.

parameterdescription.para: is the callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is not important to the driver, so it is a void pointer.
indicates one or more status events that occurred.

detaileddescription.para: Configuration table.

briefdescription.para: Compute the I3C odd-parity bit for a 7-bit dynamic address.

detaileddescription.para: Per the I3C spec and the DEV_ADDR_TABLE_LOC*n*.DEV_DYNAMIC_ADDR field, bit[7] of the 8-bit encoded value must be the odd parity of bits[6:0], i.e. parity = ~XOR(addr[6:0]). The controller transmits this 8-bit value verbatim during ENTDAA, so a raw address write with bit[7]=0 produces wrong parity for any 7-bit address that has an even number of 1-bits (e.g. 0x09, 0x0A, 0x0C, 0x0F, 0x11, 0x12, ...) and the slave NACKs the address assignment, hanging the controller.

parameterdescription.para: is the 7-bit dynamic address (only bits[6:0] are used).

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 — the odd-parity bit to place in bit[7].

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

detaileddescription.para: It waits for the response from slave and reads the response.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XI3cPsx_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xi3cpsx.h for the definition of XI3cPsx_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific XI3cPsx instance such that the driver is ready to use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is a reference to a structure containing information about a specific I3C device. This function initializes an InstancePtr object for a specific device specified by the contents of Config.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. The caller is responsible for keeping the address mapping from EffectiveAddr to the device physical base address unchanged once this function is invoked. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping changes after this function is called. If address translation is not used, use ConfigPtr->BaseAddress for this parameter, passing the physical address instead.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful. XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the instance is already initialized. XST_FAILURE if SCL clock programming or bus initialization fails (master mode only).

detaileddescription.para: Reset must only be called after the driver has been initialized. The configuration of the device after reset is the same as its configuration after initialization. Any data transfer that is in progress is aborted.
The upper layer software is responsible for re-configuring (if necessary) and reenabling interrupts for the IIC device after the reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XIicPs instance.

detaileddescription.para: Reset must only be called after the driver has been initialized. The configuration of the device after reset is the same as its configuration after initialization. Any data transfer that is in progress is aborted.
The upper layer software is responsible for re-configuring (if necessary) and reenabling interrupts for the IIC device after the reset.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XIicPs instance.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx_Cmd structure.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the send buffer.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the recv buffer.
is the number of bytes to be recv.

briefdescription.para: This function sends the transfer command.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is a pointer to the Command Info.

briefdescription.para: This function sends the Address Assignment command.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is a pointer to the Command Info.

briefdescription.para: Initializes the I3c bus by configuring the device address table, resets and assigns the dynamic address to the slave devices.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

simplesect.para: SCL timing must be programmed before calling this function. Call XI3cPsx_SetSClk() or XI3cPsx_SetSClkRate() first so that the RSTDAA and ENTDAA bus transactions issued here run at the intended clock rate. XI3cPsx_CfgInitialize() handles this sequence automatically; callers invoking XI3cPsx_BusInit() directly are responsible for calling XI3cPsx_SetSClk() first.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the status handler, which the driver calls when it encounters conditions that should be reported to the higher layer software.

detaileddescription.para: The handler executes in an interrupt context, so the amount of processing should be minimized

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XI3c instance.
FunctionPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context, so it should finish its work quickly.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates an interrupt-driven send in master mode.

detaileddescription.para: It tries to send the first FIFO-full of data, then lets the interrupt handler to handle the rest of the data if there is any.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the send buffer.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
is the instance of XI3cPsx_Cmd.

simplesect.para: This send routine is for interrupt-driven transfer only.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates an interrupt-driven receive in master mode.

detaileddescription.para: It sets the transfer size register so the slave can send data to us. The rest of the work is managed by interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the receive buffer.
is the number of bytes to be received.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx_Cmd instance.

simplesect.para: This receive routine is for interrupt-driven transfer only.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates a polled mode send in master mode.

detaileddescription.para: It sends data to the FIFO and waits for the slave to pick them up. If master fails to send data due arbitration lost, will stop transfer and with arbitration lost status If slave fails to remove data from FIFO, the send fails with time out.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the send buffer.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx_Cmd instance.

simplesect.para: This send routine is for polled mode transfer only. All the FIFO operations are in terms of words.

briefdescription.para: This function initiates a polled mode receive in master mode.

detaileddescription.para: It repeatedly sets the transfer size register so the slave can send data to us. It polls the data register for data to come in. If master fails to read data due arbitration lost, will return with arbitration lost status. If slave fails to send us data, it fails with time out.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the receive buffer.
is the number of bytes to be received.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx_Cmd instance.

simplesect.para: This receive routine is for polled mode transfer only.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for the master mode.

detaileddescription.para: It does the protocol handling for the interrupt-driven transfers.

If the Master is receiving data then the data is read from the FIFO and the Master has to request for more data (if there is more data to receive). If all the data has been received then a completion event is signalled to the upper layer by calling the callback handler. It is an error if the amount of received data is more than expected.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the device to be a slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the static address of the slave we are receiving from.

simplesect.para: Interrupt is always enabled no matter the transfer is interrupt- driven or polled mode. Whether device will be interrupted or not depends on whether the device is connected to an interrupt controller and interrupt for the device is enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function setup a slave interrupt-driven receive.

detaileddescription.para: Data processing for the receive is handled by the interrupt handler.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the receive buffer.
is the number of bytes to be received.

simplesect.para: This routine is for interrupt-driven transfer only.

briefdescription.para: This function sends a buffer in polled mode as a slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the send buffer.
is the number of bytes to be sent.
is the instance of XI3cPsx_Cmd.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if master sends us data or master terminates the transfer before all data has sent out.

simplesect.para: This send routine is for polled mode transfer only.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a buffer in polled mode as a slave.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the pointer to the receive buffer.

simplesect.para: This receive routine is for polled mode transfer only. All the FIFO operations are in terms of words.

briefdescription.para: The interrupt handler for slave mode.

detaileddescription.para: It does the protocol handling for the interrupt-driven transfers.
Completion events and errors are signaled to upper layer for proper handling.

The interrupts that are handled are:
DATA If the instance is sending, it means that the master wants to read more data from us. Send more data, and check whether we are done with this send.
If the instance is receiving, it means that the master has written more data to us. Receive more data, and check whether we are done with with this receive.
COMP This marks that stop sequence has been sent from the master, transfer is about to terminate. However, for receiving, the master may have written us some data, so receive that first.
It is an error if the amount of transferred data is less than expected.
NAK This marks that master does not want our data. It is for send only.
Other interrupts These interrupts are marked as error.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XIicPs instance.
is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, XST_FAILURE if any computed timing count falls outside [SCL_I3C_TIMING_CNT_MIN, SCL_I3C_TIMING_CNT_MAX], indicating the input clock and SCL rate combination is not representable in the hardware timing registers.
The SCL frequency is read from InstancePtr->SclkHz. To configure a custom SCL frequency, use XI3cPsx_SetSClkRate.

briefdescription.para: Sets a custom I3C SCL clock frequency by updating the instance SCL rate and configuring the hardware registers accordingly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.
is the desired SCL clock frequency in Hz.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if successful, XST_FAILURE if the requested SCL rate cannot be represented in the hardware timing registers for the given input clock.
This updates InstancePtr->SclkHz and programs the hardware. For default SCL rate set during initialization, use XI3cPsx_SetSClk instead.

briefdescription.para: Returns the SCL clock frequency currently configured in the instance.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XI3cPsx instance.

simplesect.para: The SCL clock frequency in Hz as last successfully programmed by XI3cPsx_SetSClk() or XI3cPsx_SetSClkRate().
This returns the software-stored rate, which is always kept in sync with the hardware. If XI3cPsx_SetSClkRate() fails, the previous valid rate is preserved and returned here.
